question: C-001-governance-feasibility
claim: C3-D vs erasure DO NOT collide: C3-D is a decision-NOTE (roadmap.md:53, BUILD-new decision note, effort S), NOT a registry build; erasure.md forbids only a persistent deny-list of erased ids. 3 mechanisms coexist (erasure / tombstone-sweep deleted_at / temporal-close invalid_at). Resolution: C3-D codifies the 3-way separation + guard rail (never persist hard-erased-id list); build no registry. Wave 0: spare-only -> residual(reading-b) -> C3-D note; erasure is its own GDPR packet.
